page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.6xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 processor cores operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 96.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the ecs.g5.6xlarge delivers moderate multi-core performance alongside weaker single-core capabilities. In stress-ng and Geekbench testing, multi-core CPU performance falls within the average range, whereas single-core metrics are positioned in the bottom 25%. Similarly, single-threaded tasks such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show poor performance, ranking in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks consistently register average throughput, though the Passmark memory score is categorized as weak.

Given its balanced resource allocation, the instance is suited for general-purpose workloads that can leverage multi-threaded execution, such as static web serving, software compilation, and database operations, which all achieved average benchmark ratings. It also supports light language model inference for smaller models. However, it is less optimal for single-threaded applications or memory-intensive tasks. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and standard 1:4 memory-to-vCPU ratio offer a predictable, mid-tier resource profile without the premium cost of specialized storage or accelerator hardware.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.6xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance featuring 24 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Built on the KVM hypervisor, the instance delivers 8 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core CPU capabilities and memory bandwidth, contrasted by weaker single-core and single-threaded processing speeds. This hardware profile makes the ecs.g5.6xlarge qualitatively cost-effective for balanced, multi-threaded workloads such as static web serving, database operations, and software compilation, while remaining less suited for single-threaded or memory-heavy applications.
